items.title: Jewish ANU student called 'baby killer' on campus, royal commission hears

children.content: The Royal Commission into Anti Semitism and Social Cohesion has heard universities need to improve responses when Jewish staff and students feel unsafe.

image.altText: A woman with long dark hair in a dark blazer and white shirt stands behind a microphone looking off camera.

items.title: ACT signs deal for CSIRO site, plans to build 3,000 homes

children.content: After being flagged for development over a decade ago, the ACT government has reached an agreement for the sale of land at a former CSIRO agriculture research site to construction of thousands new homes.

image.altText: A drone shot of green pastures

items.title: As the mercury drops in Canberra, cyclists are still braving the chill

children.content: Every year, winter sees many Canberrans hanging up their helmets, despite the city being considered Australia's "cycling capital." This year though, cyclist numbers have remained relatively steady, even as the mercury drops.

gallery.altText: A man rides along on a cargo-bike.

thumbnail.altText: A man rides along on a cargo-bike.

items.title: 'He gave us 10 extra years': Australian-first immune centre opens in Canberra

children.content: The Centre for Personalised Medicine at Canberra Hospital uses cutting-edge technology to identify the causes of complex immune conditions to tailor treatments for patients.

image.altText: An elderly man and his adult daughter smiling outside.

items.title: First Indigenous Military Cross recipient discovered by War Memorial

children.content: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

image.altText: A Military Cross medal in front of a line of other military medals.

items.title: Aboriginal artist recreating 'oldest living weaving practice' with glass

children.content: Lower Southern Arrernte woman Jenni Kemarre Martiniello creates glass artworks by combining a centuries-old Italian glassmaking technique with millennia-old Indigenous weaving practices.

gallery.altText: A person's hands working on a woven-patterned glass sculpture with a blowtorch.

thumbnail.altText: A person's hands working on a woven-patterned glass sculpture with a blowtorch.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: These homes are classed as 'affordable', but there's a catch

children.content: Governments across Australia are spending billions to boost the number of rentals available to people on low and middle incomes. A Four Corners investigation found many of these homes were unaffordable for those who needed them most, particularly single-income households.

gallery.altText: A composite of real estate listing photos of two modern luxury apartments.

thumbnail.altText: A composite of real estate listing photos of two modern luxury apartments.

items.title: We're back to two competing superpowers and China is pulling ahead

children.content: When it comes to AI, Australia is a spectator at a UFC cage fight between the United States and China, except the spectators are inside the cage with the combatants.

image.altText: The US flag is waving in front of the red and gold round symbol of China

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Albanese has the Coalition exactly where he wants it

children.content: When the bells resume ringing in the halls of parliament, Anthony Albanese and Angus Taylor will both do well to dispose of any complacency.

image.altText: A close up of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a white hard hat and a yellow high-vis vest.

items.title: 'He continues to inspire us': Richard Scolyer's family pay emotional tribute

children.content: Thousands have gathered for Richard Scolyer's state memorial service, with the leading melanoma pathologist remembered as an esteemed researcher and family man.

articles.title: Tour de Cure reflects on Scolyer's legacy ahead of memorial service

children.content: As Mark Beretta rode his bike up a steep Tasmanian mountain in March with all his might, it was none other than Richard Scolyer who overtook him.

image.altText: Mark Beretta and Richard Scolyer in black lyra, smiling as they stand near a paddock in Tasmania.

gallery.altText: Portrait head and shoulders Richard Scolyer slight smile wearing a suit

thumbnail.altText: Portrait head and shoulders Richard Scolyer slight smile wearing a suit

items.title: What happened when a rare bird found a place to nest near a logging coupe

children.content: It was late 2021 when one of Australia's rarest birds started to arrive in the native forests of Lonnavale. Ecologists had expected it but for a reacting forestry sector, it was "not sustainable".

image.altText: A fledgling swift parrot pokes its head out of a tree hollow.

items.title: Molly Ticehurst's killer's sentencing brought forward

children.content: The sentencing of the man who murdered New South Wales woman Molly Ticehurst has been brought forward to allow a psychiatrist to give evidence.

image.altText: A woman smiles at the camera.

items.title: How Lindsey Graham's death could affect Donald Trump's agenda

children.content: US senator Lindsey Graham died suddenly on Saturday night, local time, creating an unexpected vacancy in Congress. Here's what we know about what comes next.

gallery.altText: Lindsey Graham talks in front of a gold-domed tower of Kyiv's Saint Sophia Cathedral.

thumbnail.altText: Lindsey Graham talks in front of a gold-domed tower of Kyiv's Saint Sophia Cathedral.

items.title: Former National Trust director unfairly dismissed, Fair Work Commission finds

children.content: The Fair Work Commission has found Scott Carlin, former managing director of the National Trust of Tasmania, was unfairly dismissed just two months before his two-year contract was set to expire.

image.altText: A man wearing a hat smiles at the camera.

items.title: Bazball: An uncomplicated idea leaving a very complex legacy

children.content: Brendon McCullum deliberately set out to make England's men's Test team a less complicated team to play cricket for. After four years of Bazball's highs and lows, it leaves a very complicated legacy.

image.altText: Brendon McCullum looks up

items.title: New exhibition challenges visitors to slow down and put their phones away

children.content: A new show at Hawthorn Arts Centre offers a range of immersive artworks made from books and paper that demand, and reward, our full attention.

gallery.altText: Palomita Soledad by Nadia Hernandez featuring large cutout letters adhered to a large canvas, hanging in a gallery.

thumbnail.altText: Palomita Soledad by Nadia Hernandez featuring large cutout letters adhered to a large canvas, hanging in a gallery.

items.title: History quiz: Test your recall on Tay Tay and the Tour de France

children.content: How much do you know about long-past British monarchs, the Salem witch trials and Nintendo games of the 80s? Find out with this week's quiz.

image.altText: Close up of Taylor Swift looking serious on stage in sparkly bodysuit

items.title: How setting small, specific goals can help reduce your drinking

children.content: If you’ve fallen off the Dry July wagon or you just aren’t interested in teetotalling, trying smaller steps to reducing your alcohol intake can still make a difference to your health.

image.altText: A group of young people drinking and laughing

items.title: Floods devastate Bangladesh as dozens killed

children.content: Days of torrential monsoon rain have triggered major flooding, leaving millions stranded and facing food supply shortages in the south-east of the country.

gallery.altText: Residents wade through a waterlogged street after heavy monsoon rains in Dhaka, Bangladesh.

thumbnail.altText: Residents wade through a waterlogged street after heavy monsoon rains in Dhaka, Bangladesh.

items.title: ASX slips as oil price jumps, Telstra warns of post-outage scams

children.content: The ASX has slipped on renewed war tensions, while Telstra has warned customers about a spate of postage scamming. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Israel to vote in October as Netanyahu support slips amid controversies

children.content: Israelis will head to the polls on the last Tuesday in October for an election that will serve as a referendum on the legacy of the country's longest-serving prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu.

image.altText: Benjamin Netanyah dressed in black walks among a crowd of people.

items.title: Bangkok pub fire kills at least 27 people and injures 63 others

children.content: A huge fire engulfed a pub in Bangkok, killing at least 27 people before firefighters brought the blaze under control.

image.altText: Rescuers stand outside a closed pub with a bright, colourful facade and the words 'Blend 285' on the front.

items.title: FIFA boss hints at World Cup expansion talks, defends hydration breaks

children.content: FIFA president Gianni Infantino believes this year's World Cup is proof that expanding the tournament was the right move.

image.altText: Gianni Infantino smiles and points with his right hand

items.title: Camp to boost female firefighter participation ditched after one year

children.content: The free camp ran over a weekend in June 2024 for students in years 9 and 10, exploring firefighting activities, road crashes, swiftwater rescues, and physical fitness training.

gallery.altText: A close-up of a fire truck, with 'fire' written on red paint

thumbnail.altText: A close-up of a fire truck, with 'fire' written on red paint

avatar.alt: A portrait shot of Eden standing next to a tree.

items.title: Government urged to 'bite the bullet' and mandate mobile phone roaming

children.content: Already available to international tourists, consumer advocates want to see mobile roaming mandated for domestic customers too.

image.altText: woman in country hat holding phone to ear as sun sets

items.title: Melbourne cafe catches fire three days after alleged drive-by shooting

children.content: Police are treating a fire at an Essendon North cafe as suspicious, just days after the business was sprayed with bullets.

image.altText: Police tape seen at an Essendon North cafe.

items.title: Worn-down 'jewel' needs some love ahead of World Heritage Bid

children.content: Once a booming gold rush town, Walhalla is preparing for a different kind of boom, but first, it needs critical government investment in infrastructure.

image.altText: A small village of heritage buildings, surrounded by autumn trees, seen from up high

items.title: Agonising finish sees Lee denied another Scottish Open title

children.content: Min Woo Lee comes close to emulating his feats from 2021 but is denied by a bogey-free run-in from eventual winner Tom Kim.

image.altText: Min Woo Lee holds a golf ball in his right hand

items.title: Sinner triumphs over Zverev for second consecutive Wimbledon win

children.content: The top-ranked Jannik Sinner beat Alexander Zverev for his second consecutive title at Wimbledon after his German opponent appeared bothered by a knee issue.

articles.title: Hewitt drops Wimbledon boys' title after epic battle against US star

children.content: Cruz Hewitt comes up short in his bid to win the Wimbledon boys' title, being beaten by Jordan Lee in the final.

image.altText: Cruz Hewitt looking frustrated while holding his racquet

articles.title: Nosková downs 'ex-friend' Muchová to win all-Czech Wimbledon final

children.content: Linda Nosková becomes the third Czech woman in four years to claim the Wimbledon singles title as she downs her great friend in the final.

image.altText: A young woman beams as she holds a giant sterling silver plate in front of a crowd.

items.title: McCullum 'gutted' after being sacked as England's Test coach

children.content: The architect of England's controversial Bazball approach to Test cricket is given his marching orders after a disappointing string of results.

image.altText: Brendon McCullum walks with his head down

items.title: Why are the world's biggest AI firms so attracted to the NT?

children.content: The NT's sunny skies, natural gas reserves and proximity to Asia have caught the attention of a dozen data centre developers.

image.altText: A computer-generated image of a huge industrial site in an outback area.

items.title: The 'stupid video' this AFLW star can't get away from

children.content: AFLW star Zarlie Goldsworthy used to carry the losses as GWS languished down the bottom of the ladder. She's learnt to let them go and stay motived.
